content:
# Rotating Disk Apparatus

**Summary**: A laboratory equipment used to study the kinetics of chemical reactions between fluids and solid surfaces under controlled flow conditions.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-khalid|Khalid, 2014]] used Rotating Disk Apparatus to conduct reaction kinetics measurement for dolomite samples.

- [[2017-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2017]] used rotating-disk-apparatus to study the kinetics of GLDA, DTPA, and EDTA with carbonate samples.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2017-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2017]] used rotating-disk-apparatus to study the kinetics of GLDA, DTPA, and EDTA with carbonate samples.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Weight loss values increased as disk rotational speed increased from 250 to 1250 rpm. ([[2014-khalid|Khalid, 2014, p. 142]])

- Determined that DTPA/SW was mass transfer limited due to high salt content in seawater. ([[2017-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2017, p. 132]])
